---
title: "NBL1Manuscript_Figure6"
author: "Courtney Willey"
date: "11/10/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Code Script for Nbl1 Manuscript: NBL1 Correlates with Renal Phenotypes in Mouse Models of Kidney Disease, but is Not Causal.

# Figure 6. Quantitative Trait Loci Analysis of NBL1 Concentration in DO-XLAS Mice.
QTL mapping of serum NBL1 concentration at 15-weeks in DO-XLAS Cohorts 1 and 2. Allele effect plot for Chr. 4 Locus in females. Correlation between kidney Nbl1 expression and serum NBL1 concentration (RankZ Transformed) in females and males from DO-XLAS Cohorts 1 and 2.

# Description
This is an R Markdown document for the 2022-01 project using Diversity Outbred 
(DO) mice with X-Linked Alport Syndrome (XLAS). This document specifically 
obtains QTLs for NBL1 analysis. 

```{r Install, include=FALSE}
#Install Packages.
install.packages("qtl2") 
install.packages("devtools") 
install.packages("knitr") 
install.packages("qtl2convert") 
install.packages("ggplot2") 
install.packages("dplyr") 
install.packages("tibble") 
install.packages("data.table")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("multcomp")
install.packages("readxl")
```

```{r Load, include=FALSE}
#Load Packages.
library("qtl2") 
library("devtools") 
library("knitr") 
library("qtl2convert") 
library("ggplot2") 
library("dplyr") 
library("tibble") 
library("data.table")
library("tidyverse")
library("multcomp")
library("readxl")
```

# Starting with QTL Analysis in Cohorts 1 and 2.

# Load Files
First, let's load in the files we will be using.
```{r Files}
# Load in the GRCm39 mouse reference genome.
markers<-read.delim("/projects/korstanje-lab/RNAseq/DOXLASCohort2/NOVOmar2024/ref.genome_grid.GRCm39.tsv")  

# Load in the probability file from 22-01, that has been obtained using GRCm39.
probs<-readRDS("/projects/korstanje-lab/RNAseq/DOXLASCohort2/NOVOmar2024/Results/MergedProbs2.rds") 
#Fp = apply(probs, 1:2, mean)
#image(Fp) 

# Load in the phenotype and covariate data file for each sex.
phenoF <-read_csv("/projects/korstanje-lab/RNAseq/DOXLASCohort2/NOVOmar2024/Phenotype/2201CombinedCohortFemales.csv")
phenoM <-read_csv("/projects/korstanje-lab/RNAseq/DOXLASCohort2/NOVOmar2024/Phenotype/2201CombinedCohortMales.csv")
```

# Load in Phenotypes
Because all of our phenotypes and covariates are within the same .csv file, we 
need to make individual data matrices for each phenotype.

```{r Phenotypes}
# Load in just Female NBL1.
FemaleNBL1 = phenoF[c("nbl1_15w_rankz")]
FemaleNBL1 = data.matrix(FemaleNBL1)
is.numeric(FemaleNBL1)

# Load in just Male NBL1.
MaleNBL1 = phenoM[c("nbl1_15w_rankz")]
MaleNBL1 = data.matrix(MaleNBL1)
is.numeric(MaleNBL1)
```

# Load in Covariates
We also need to make individual data matrices for each covariate.

```{r Covariates}
# Female NBL1 Covariates.
NBL1_covarF = phenoF[c("cohort", "sire_generation")] 
NBL1_covarF = data.matrix(NBL1_covarF)
is.numeric(NBL1_covarF)

# Male NBL1 Covariates.
NBL1_covarM = phenoM[c("cohort", "sire_generation")] 
NBL1_covarM = data.matrix(NBL1_covarM)
is.numeric(NBL1_covarM)
```

# Extract Samples
Now we need to now extract the samples that exist between our phenotype and 
probabilities files.

```{r Samples}
# Pull sample IDs from each file.
phenoIDsF <- rownames(phenoF)
phenoIDsM <- rownames(phenoM)
probsIDs <- rownames(probs)

# Determine which sample IDs exist in both files for Females.
samplesF = intersect(phenoIDsF, probsIDs)
probsF = probs[samplesF,,]

# Determine which sample IDs exist in both files for Males.
samplesM = intersect(phenoIDsM, probsIDs)
probsM = probs[samplesM,,]
```

# QTL Prep
However, before we can run our QTL code, we need to obtain the calculated 
probabilities (LOD scores), kinship information, and create a map.

```{r QTL}
# Convert DOQTL Code to qtl2 for Females. 
qtl2probsF = probs_doqtl_to_qtl2(probsF, markers)
aprF <- genoprob_to_alleleprob(qtl2probsF)

# Convert DOQTL Code to qtl2 for Males. 
qtl2probsM = probs_doqtl_to_qtl2(probsM, markers)
aprM <- genoprob_to_alleleprob(qtl2probsM)

# Run kinship matrix for Females.
kF <- calc_kinship(aprF,"loco")
#options(na.action = 'na.pass') #Leave in NA's.

#Run kinship matrix for Males.
kM <- calc_kinship(aprM,"loco")
#options(na.action = 'na.pass') #Leave in NA's.

# Make Map for Females and Males. 
pmap = qtl2convert::map_df_to_list(markers, chr_column = "chr", pos_column = "pos", marker_column = "marker")
```

# Nbl1 QTL & Allele Effects
We can run the scan1 function and plot the results for our Neuroblastoma 
Supressor of Tumorgenicity 1 (Nbl1) phenotype, which is a potential modifier 
gene for Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) and possibly XLAS.

```{r Nbl1}
# Scan1 for Female Nbl1 QTL at 15w of age with DO generation, and cohort as an additive covariate.  
nbl1_qtl_add_F = scan1(pheno = FemaleNBL1, genoprobs = aprF, kinship = kF, addcovar = NBL1_covarF)

# Scan1 for Male Nbl1 QTL at 15w of age with DO generation, and cohort as an additive covariate. 
nbl1_qtl_add_M = scan1(pheno = MaleNBL1, genoprobs = aprM, kinship = kM, addcovar = NBL1_covarM)

# Plotting the results.
plot(nbl1_qtl_add_F, pmap,1,main="Female NBL1 15 weeks")
plot(nbl1_qtl_add_M, pmap,1,main="Male NBL1 15 weeks")

# Checking heritability of NBL1 for Females
k_heritF <- calc_kinship(aprF, "overall")
hsqF <- est_herit(FemaleNBL1, k_heritF, addcovar = NBL1_covarF)
hsqF
# A heritability of 0.74 is considered high, suggesting genetic factors play a strong role in females.

# Checking heritability of NBL1 for Males
k_heritM <- calc_kinship(aprM, "overall")
hsqM <- est_herit(MaleNBL1, k_heritM, addcovar = NBL1_covarM)
hsqM
# This heritability is barely moderate, suggesting both genetic and environmental factors contribute in males.


# Create permutations for significance testing.
FemalePerms_Nbl1_1000 <- scan1perm(pheno = FemaleNBL1,  genoprobs = aprF, kinship = kF, addcovar = NBL1_covarF, n_perm = 1000)
#summary(FemalePerms_Nbl1_1000,alpha=c(0.2,0.05))
#According to the permutations, the LOD Thresholds for Females are as follows: 
#Sex    Nbl115WK_Female_Rankz 
#0.2     6.85      
#0.05    7.65         

MalePerms_Nbl1_1000 <- scan1perm(pheno = MaleNBL1,  genoprobs = aprM, kinship = kM, addcovar = NBL1_covarM, n_perm = 1000)
#According to the permutations, the LOD Thresholds for Males are as follows: 
#Sex    Nbl115WK_Male_Rankz 
#0.2     6.75      
#0.05    7.58    
pdf("NBL1_15wMale_QTL.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)


# Plot with Perms.
pdf("NBL1_15wFemale_QTL.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
perms <- plot(nbl1_qtl_add_F, pmap,1,main="Female NBL1 15 weeks (1000 permutations)",ylim=c(0,8.00))+abline(h=6.85,col="orange")+abline(h=7.65,col="red")
dev.off()

pdf("NBL1_15wMale_QTL.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
plot(nbl1_qtl_add_M, pmap,1,main="Male NBL1 15 weeks (1000 permutations)",ylim=c(0,8.00))+abline(h=6.75,col="orange")+abline(h=7.58,col="red")
dev.off()

# Find peaks.
find_peaks(scan1_output = nbl1_qtl_add_F, map = pmap, threshold = 6.85, prob = 0.95, expand2markers = FALSE)
find_peaks(scan1_output = nbl1_qtl_add_M, map = pmap, threshold = 6.75, prob = 0.95, expand2markers = FALSE)
# No peaks pass threshold in males.

# Allele Effects in Females
Pheno4 = subset.data.frame(FemaleNBL1, select="nbl1_15w_rankz")
chr4_blup <- scan1blup(genoprobs = qtl2probs[,"4"], pheno = Pheno4, addcovar = NBL1_covarF)
plot_coefCC(chr4_blup, pmap, scan1_output = nbl1_qtl_add_F[ drop = FALSE],legend="bottomleft")
# Define region of interest on chromosome 4 (e.g., #from 50 Mb to 100 Mb)
start_pos <- 125e6 # in Mb
end_pos <- 160e6 # in Mb
# Subset the pmap for chromosome 4
chr4_pmap <- pmap[["4"]]
region_markers <- names(chr4_pmap)[chr4_pmap >= start_pos & chr4_pmap <= end_pos]
# Subset the BLUP results to the region
chr4_blup_zoom <- chr4_blup[region_markers, , drop = FALSE]
# Subset the pmap to the same region
chr4_pmap_zoom <- chr4_pmap[region_markers]

# Plot the zoomed-in region on Chr.4 in Females
pdf("NBL1_15wFemale_Chr4AlleleEffects.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
plot_coefCC(chr4_blup_zoom, list("4" = chr4_pmap_zoom),scan1_output = nbl1_qtl_add_F[region_markers, , drop = FALSE],legend = "bottomleft")
dev.off()
```

# Looking at correlation between kidney Nbl1 expression and serum NBL1 concentration (RankZ Transformed) in females and males from DO-XLAS Cohorts 1 and 2.

```{r Correlation}
# Load File
CombinedCohort <- read_excel("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TheJacksonLaboratory/Korstanje Lab/20-01 DO-XLAS/20-01 Assays/Combined Distributions/Normalized Gene Expression/Cohort1&2_NormalizedGeneExpression_NBL1.xlsx")

# Subset 15w Females & Males
cohort12F <- CombinedCohort[CombinedCohort$sex == 0, ]
cohort12M <- CombinedCohort[CombinedCohort$sex == 1, ]

cohort12F$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort <- as.numeric(cohort12F$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort)
cohort12F$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120 <- as.numeric(cohort12F$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120)

cohort12M$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort <- as.numeric(cohort12M$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort)
cohort12M$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120 <- as.numeric(cohort12M$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120)

# Looking at NBL1 for Females in Both Cohorts
CorrelationF <- ggplot(cohort12F, mapping=aes(x=as.numeric(Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120),y=as.numeric(Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort), size=1))+geom_point() + xlim(min(cohort12F$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120), max(1.)) + ylim(min(cohort12F$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort), max(cohort12F$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort)) +scale_color_manual(values=c("purple"))+geom_smooth(method="lm",color="blue", se = FALSE, size=0.5)+xlab("RankZ (Nbl1 Expression)")+ylab("RankZ (serum NBL1)")+ggtitle("Correlation Between NBL1 Levels and Nbl1 Expression in Females")
CorrelationF

# Determining if this is significant
SignificanceF <- cor.test(as.numeric(cohort12F$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120), as.numeric(cohort12F$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort))
view(SignificanceF)
# p-value = 0.0006432811, SIGNIFICANT
R2F <- SignificanceF$estimate^2
R2F #0.06456162

# Looking at NBL1 for Males
CorrelationM <- ggplot(cohort12M, mapping=aes(x=as.numeric(Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120),y=as.numeric(Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort), size=1))+geom_point() + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.0, 1.5), breaks = c(0.0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5)) + ylim(min(cohort12M$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort), max(cohort12M$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort)) +scale_color_manual(values=c("purple"))+geom_smooth(method="lm",color="blue", se = FALSE, size=0.5)+xlab("RankZ (Nbl1 Expression)")+ylab("RankZ (serum NBL1)")+ggtitle("Correlation Between NBL1 Levels and Nbl1 Expression in Males")
CorrelationM

# Determining if this is significant
SignificanceM <- cor.test(as.numeric(cohort12M$Nbl1_ENSMUSG00000041120), as.numeric(cohort12M$Nbl1_15w_Rankz_CombinedCohort))
view(SignificanceM)
# p-value = 0.3102138, SIGNIFICANT
R2M <- SignificanceM$estimate^2
R2M #0.005535888
```
